Import ListNotations.
Require Import Stdlib.Strings.Ascii.
Axiom todo : forall {A : Type}, A.

(* === Regular expression type === *)

Inductive reg_exp (T : Type) : Type :=
| EmptySet
| EmptyStr
| Char (t : T)
| App (r1 r2 : reg_exp T)
| Union (r1 r2 : reg_exp T)
| Star (r : reg_exp T).

Arguments EmptySet {T}.
Arguments EmptyStr {T}.
Arguments Char {T} _.
Arguments App {T} _ _.
Arguments Union {T} _ _.
Arguments Star {T} _.

(* === Match relation === *)

current_paradigm.idea: Prove the cons-case decomposition lemmas (app_ne and star_ne) first using inversion and existing existence lemmas
current_paradigm.description: Target the admitted helper lemmas app_ne and star_ne before implementing derive: these lemmas characterize how a cons string matches App and Star. For app_ne, start from app_exists specialized to a :: s and use case analysis on the left split to separate [] vs nonempty, pushing the nonempty head into the left match to form a :: s0 =~ re0; conversely, construct the appropriate MApp proof from either branch. For star_ne, use the Star constructors: show that any cons-match must be of the form MStarApp with a nonempty first chunk, giving a :: s0 =~ re and s1 =~ Star re; conversely, build MStarApp to witness the backward direction. With these lemmas Qed, the later derive_corr proof becomes almost mechanical: each regex constructor reduces to propositional combinations that app_ne and star_ne directly decompose, while union uses union_disj and char/empty cases use provided lemmas. This approach reduces admitted proofs first, making subsequent correctness proofs shorter and more robust.
current_paradigm.what_to_optimize: combined_score by decreasing the number of admitted lemmas early, improving the denominator while preparing ground for an easy derive_corr proof later
current_paradigm.cautions: Be precise with list equalities when using app_exists; destruct concatenations carefully to avoid impossible cases (discriminate when [] = a :: _); avoid introducing new helper lemmas—stick to inversion and existing equivalences; ensure both directions (<->) are handled for each lemma

active_paradigms.idea: Implement Brzozowski derivative structurally for derive and prove correctness by structural induction
Prove the cons-case decomposition lemmas (app_ne and star_ne) first using inversion and existing existence lemmas

current_paradigm.idea: Implement Brzozowski derivative structurally for derive and prove correctness by structural induction
current_paradigm.description: Define derive using the standard Brzozowski rules by structural recursion on the regex: EmptySet and EmptyStr map to EmptySet; Char c maps to EmptyStr if a=c else EmptySet; Union distributes derivative over both sides; App uses Union (App (derive a r1) r2) with a conditional branch If match_eps r1 then derive a r2 else EmptySet; Star maps to App (derive a r) (Star r). Keep the implementation normalization-free (no simplification) to avoid introducing extra obligations. Then prove app_ne and star_ne using the provided app_exists and the Star constructors: prove the forward and backward directions by inversion and by constructing witnesses, respectively. With these in place, prove derive_corr by induction on re with s arbitrary, using existing lemmas (union_disj, app_ne, star_ne, match_eps_refl) to discharge each constructor case. In the App case, use app_ne to split the cons-case and match_eps_refl to connect the boolean branch to [] =~ r1. In the Star case, use star_ne to peel one iteration. This plan reduces the single todo (derive) and systematically removes the admitted lemmas in subsequent iterations without adding new todos.
current_paradigm.what_to_optimize: combined_score by first eliminating the single todo (derive) with a concrete term, then proving derive_corr in follow-ups using existing lemmas; minimize introducing new sub-lemmas to keep admitted count low
current_paradigm.cautions: Do not attempt to normalize unions/apps inside derive to avoid new helper functions; use reflect from match_eps_refl only to justify the App case’s conditional; ensure no dependence on external plugins; keep proofs constructor-specific and avoid dependent destruction that could complicate goals; maintain the exact statement of existing lemmas

active_paradigms.idea: Implement Brzozowski derivative structurally for derive and prove correctness by structural induction
Prove the cons-case decomposition lemmas (app_ne and star_ne) first using inversion and existing existence lemmas

current_paradigm.idea: Add smart constructors to normalize EmptySet/EmptyStr during derive to simplify proofs and case splits
current_paradigm.description: Introduce small helper functions union_s, app_s, and maybe_s that absorb EmptySet/EmptyStr cases: union_s x EmptySet = x, union_s EmptySet y = y; app_s EmptyStr r = r, app_s r EmptyStr = r when appropriate for App contexts that represent concatenation with epsilon; otherwise return App. Use these smart constructors inside derive so that its output eliminates trivial EmptySet/EmptyStr branches early. This reduces the number of proof subcases in derive_corr, often allowing direct application of MApp and union_disj without auxiliary contradictions. Prove simple correctness lemmas for each smart constructor (e.g., s =~ union_s r1 r2 <-> s =~ Union r1 r2) which are trivial via union_disj and empty_matches_eps. Then proceed to prove app_ne and star_ne as before, and use the simplified derive in the main proof to streamline the App case, especially when match_eps r1 holds.
current_paradigm.what_to_optimize: combined_score by making derive_corr shorter and more automatable, thereby increasing likelihood to discharge all Admitted proofs cleanly
current_paradigm.cautions: Keep smart constructors total and conservative: they must be extensionally equivalent to the raw constructors. Do not attempt aggressive algebraic normalization (like associativity/commutativity), only absorb EmptySet/EmptyStr. Ensure smart constructors do not introduce new todos; if auxiliary lemmas are added, keep them minimal and prove them immediately. Maintain the one-change-per-iteration rule by first replacing the todo with a version of derive that calls the smart constructors, then proving their equivalences in subsequent steps before tackling derive_corr.

tried_paradigms_summary.idea: Implement Brzozowski derivative structurally for derive and prove correctness by structural induction
Prove the cons-case decomposition lemmas (app_ne and star_ne) first using inversion and existing existence lemmas

active_paradigms.idea: Implement Brzozowski derivative via a structural Fixpoint and prove soundness by structural induction
Add smart constructors to normalize EmptySet/EmptyStr during derive to simplify proofs and case splits
